seoTitle: Starrystone Tech Solutions - Power Supply & LED Lighting Applications
seoDescription: Explore Starrystone Tech application solutions for AC-DC power supplies, LED lighting systems, and DC-DC conversion. LiTong provides complete design support.

faqs.question: What application solutions does Starrystone Tech provide?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech provides comprehensive solutions for power management applications. Their solutions cover AC-DC power supplies for adapters and chargers, LED lighting systems for general and commercial lighting, DC-DC conversion for industrial and consumer electronics, and gate drive solutions for motor controls and power inverters. Each solution includes reference designs, BOM recommendations, and application guidance to accelerate product development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solution categories to find the right application for your project. Contact us for detailed design support.

faqs.question: How can LiTong support my Starrystone Tech-based design?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Starrystone Tech-based designs including reference designs and application notes, schematic and PCB layout review, component selection guidance, sample provision for evaluation, technical troubleshooting, and production support. Our FAE team has extensive experience with Starrystone Tech products and can help optimize your design for performance, cost, and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design phase for optimal support. We can provide reference designs and review your implementation.

faqs.question: What is included in Starrystone Tech reference designs?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech reference designs include complete schematics with component values, PCB layout files and guidelines, BOM with recommended suppliers, application notes explaining design choices, test reports with performance data, and efficiency measurements. These reference designs are tested and validated, providing a solid foundation for your product development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from our team. They provide an excellent starting point for your development.

faqs.question: Can Starrystone Tech solutions be customized for specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Starrystone Tech solutions can be customized for specific application requirements. Modifications may include adjusting output voltage and current levels, optimizing for specific efficiency targets, adapting for different form factors, integrating additional features, and modifying protection thresholds. Our FAE team can guide you through customization options while maintaining reliability and compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Discuss your specific requirements with our FAE team. We can recommend appropriate customizations.

faqs.question: What certifications do Starrystone Tech reference designs meet?
faqs.answer: Starrystone Tech reference designs are developed with certification requirements in mind. They typically comply with energy efficiency standards (DoE Level VI, CoC Tier 2), safety standards for the target markets, and EMI/EMC requirements. However, final product certification is the responsibility of the product manufacturer. We provide guidance on meeting certification requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for certification early in design. We can provide guidance on meeting relevant standards.

solutions.name: High-Efficiency Power Adapter
solutions.title: High-Efficiency Power Adapter Solution
solutions.description: Complete power adapter solution using Starrystone Tech PWM controller and synchronous rectifier for high efficiency and low standby power.
solutions.longDescription:
The High-Efficiency Power Adapter from starrystonetech represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of starrystonetech components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: High efficiency up to 90% reduces energy consumption and thermal losses
Low standby power below 75mW meets latest efficiency standards
Compact design with minimal external components reduces BOM cost
Comprehensive protection ensures safety and reliability
Proven reference design accelerates time to market

coreAdvantages.title: High Integration Reduces BOM
coreAdvantages.description: Integrated 650V MOSFET in PWM controller and intelligent synchronous rectification minimize external component count, reducing PCB size and BOM cost.

coreAdvantages.title: Excellent Efficiency Performance
coreAdvantages.description: Quasi-resonant operation combined with synchronous rectification achieves up to 90% efficiency and meets stringent DoE Level VI and CoC Tier 2 standards.

coreAdvantages.title: Low EMI Design
coreAdvantages.description: Valley switching and soft switching techniques reduce EMI, simplifying EMI filter design and reducing filter component cost.

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Protection
coreAdvantages.description: Built-in over-voltage, over-current, over-temperature, and short-circuit protection ensures safe operation under all conditions.

coreAdvantages.title: Fast Time to Market
coreAdvantages.description: Complete reference design with tested and validated performance reduces development time and risk.

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed a cost-effective, high-efficiency power adapter solution for a new line of USB-C chargers. Their existing design used discrete components from multiple vendors, resulting in high BOM cost and complex manufacturing. They required a solution that could achieve 90% efficiency and meet DoE Level VI standards while maintaining competitive pricing.
customerCases.solution: We recommended the Starrystone Tech STR-A6069H + STR-SR3010 solution, providing a highly integrated design with minimal external components. The reference design was customized for 5V/3A USB-C output with PD protocol support. Our FAE team provided schematic review and PCB layout guidance to optimize thermal performance and EMI.
result: Achieved 90.5% efficiency at full load
Standby power measured at 68mW (below 75mW requirement)
Reduced BOM cost by 25% compared to discrete solution
Passed EMI testing on first attempt
Successfully launched with 300K units shipped in first year
customerCases.feedback: The Starrystone Tech solution exceeded our efficiency targets while reducing cost. LiTong's technical support was instrumental in optimizing our design for manufacturing. The reference design saved us months of development time.

customerCases.challenge: The customer required a reliable 24W adapter for networking equipment with strict thermal constraints due to sealed enclosure. The design needed to operate at 50°C ambient with minimal heat sinking and maintain high efficiency across wide load variations (20-100%).
customerCases.solution: We proposed a design using STR-A6069H with optimized synchronous rectification. The design utilized low-RDS(on) MOSFETs and optimized transformer for minimal losses. Our team provided thermal simulation and layout optimization to maximize heat dissipation in the constrained space.
result: Operated reliably at 50°C ambient without external heat sink
Achieved 89% average efficiency across load range
Surface temperature remained below 75°C at full load
Passed 1000-hour reliability testing
Design adopted as standard platform for multiple products
customerCases.feedback: LiTong's expertise in thermal optimization was crucial to meeting our challenging requirements. The Starrystone Tech solution delivers consistent performance even under harsh thermal conditions.

faeInsights.content: The STR-A6069H + STR-SR3010 combination represents an optimal solution for modern power adapter designs requiring high efficiency and low cost. The key to success with this solution is proper transformer design - the transformer affects efficiency, EMI, and regulation performance. I recommend working with qualified transformer suppliers who understand flyback design. For synchronous rectification, MOSFET selection is critical - choose devices with low RDS(on) and appropriate voltage rating (typically 40-60V for 5V output). The gate drive characteristics of STR-SR3010 work well with most logic-level MOSFETs. Pay special attention to PCB layout - keep the switching loop areas small, place input filter capacitors close to the rectifier, and ensure adequate copper area for thermal management. The feedback loop compensation should be designed for stable operation across all load conditions.
faeInsights.logic: The design decision framework for adapter development follows this sequence: First, determine output power and voltage requirements based on application needs. Second, calculate transformer turns ratio and inductance based on input voltage range and switching frequency. Third, select synchronous rectifier MOSFET with appropriate RDS(on) for target efficiency. Fourth, design EMI filter based on conducted emission requirements. Fifth, optimize thermal management considering enclosure constraints. Sixth, implement feedback loop with proper compensation for stable regulation. Finally, validate through efficiency testing, thermal testing, and EMI scanning.
keyTakeaways: Transformer design is critical for efficiency and EMI performance
Synchronous rectifier MOSFET selection significantly impacts efficiency
PCB layout optimization reduces EMI and improves thermal performance
Feedback loop stability must be verified across all operating conditions
Pre-compliance EMI testing saves time in certification
commonPitfalls: Inadequate transformer design leading to poor efficiency or saturation
Improper synchronous MOSFET selection causing excessive losses
Insufficient EMI filtering resulting in certification failures
Poor thermal design causing derating under load
Feedback loop instability causing output voltage oscillation
bestPractices: Work with experienced transformer suppliers for optimal design
Select synchronous MOSFETs with adequate margin in voltage and current ratings
Implement proper EMI filtering from the start of design
Design thermal management for worst-case operating conditions
Validate feedback loop stability with load step testing

faqs.question: What is the maximum efficiency achievable with this solution?
faqs.answer: The STR-A6069H + STR-SR3010 solution can achieve up to 90-91% efficiency at full load for 5V output applications. The actual efficiency depends on transformer design, synchronous MOSFET selection, and operating conditions. At 115VAC input, efficiency is typically 89-90%. At 230VAC input, efficiency can reach 90-91% due to lower primary current. Light load efficiency is also excellent due to frequency foldback and burst mode operation. The synchronous rectifier contributes 3-5% efficiency improvement compared to diode rectification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 89-91% efficiency depending on input voltage and design optimization. Contact us for efficiency optimization guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the transformer for this design?
faqs.answer: Transformer selection involves several parameters: Core size (EE16 for up to 30W), turns ratio (based on input/output voltages), primary inductance (calculated for desired switching frequency), and winding construction (for low leakage inductance and good coupling). The turns ratio is typically 15-20:1 for 5V output from universal input. Primary inductance of 1-2mH is typical for 65kHz operation. Work with transformer suppliers who can provide designs optimized for your specific requirements. Starrystone Tech can recommend qualified suppliers.
faqs.decisionGuide: EE16 core for up to 30W. Work with qualified suppliers. Contact us for recommendations.

faqs.question: What EMI considerations are important for this design?
faqs.answer: EMI design considerations include: Input filter design with common mode choke and X/Y capacitors; Proper PCB layout with minimized switching loops; Snubber circuits across primary MOSFET and secondary rectifier; Shielding techniques if needed; and Grounding design to reduce common mode noise. The quasi-resonant operation of STR-A6069H inherently reduces EMI compared to fixed-frequency PWM. Conducted EMI testing should be performed early to identify any issues before finalizing the design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design EMI filter from the start. Test early to identify issues. We can provide EMI design guidance.

faqs.question: How do I optimize thermal performance?
faqs.answer: Thermal optimization involves: Selecting low-loss components (especially synchronous MOSFET); Designing adequate copper area for heat spreading; Using thermal vias to inner layers; Positioning heat-generating components for airflow; and Considering enclosure design for heat dissipation. The synchronous rectifier MOSFET typically generates the most heat - select a device with low RDS(on) and adequate package thermal capability. Test under worst-case conditions (maximum load, highest ambient temperature) to verify thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Focus on synchronous MOSFET selection and PCB thermal design. Test under worst-case conditions.

faqs.question: What safety certifications are required for power adapters?
faqs.answer: Power adapters typically require multiple safety certifications: UL 60950-1 or UL 62368-1 for USA, CE marking with EN 60950-1 or EN 62368-1 for Europe, CCC for China, and PSE for Japan. Energy efficiency certifications include DoE Level VI for USA and CoC Tier 2 for Europe. The design should incorporate appropriate creepage and clearance distances, isolation barriers, and protection against electric shock. Starrystone Tech reference designs incorporate safety considerations, but final certification is the manufacturer's responsibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for required certifications early. Engage with testing labs during development. We can provide guidance.

faqs.question: Can this solution be modified for different output voltages?
faqs.answer: Yes, the solution can be adapted for different output voltages (typically 5V to 24V). Modifications include: Adjusting transformer turns ratio for the new output voltage; Selecting appropriate synchronous rectifier MOSFET voltage rating; Updating feedback resistor divider for voltage regulation; Adjusting output capacitor voltage rating; and Potentially modifying snubber circuits. Higher output voltages may require different transformer core sizes. Our FAE team can provide guidance for specific voltage modifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Solution can be adapted for 5V-24V outputs. Contact us for specific voltage modifications.

solutions.name: LED Lighting Driver System
solutions.title: LED Lighting Driver System Solution
solutions.description: Complete LED lighting solution using Starrystone Tech LED drivers for high-efficiency, reliable illumination in general and commercial lighting applications.
solutions.longDescription:
The LED Lighting Driver System from starrystonetech represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of starrystonetech components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: High efficiency up to 96% reduces energy consumption and heat generation
Constant current regulation ensures consistent LED brightness and long lifetime
Multiple dimming options enable smart lighting control
Comprehensive protection features ensure safe and reliable operation
Compact design fits various lighting fixture form factors

coreAdvantages.title: High Efficiency Reduces Heat
coreAdvantages.description: Up to 96% efficiency minimizes power losses, reducing heat generation in the driver and simplifying thermal management in lighting fixtures.

coreAdvantages.title: Precise Current Regulation
coreAdvantages.description: ±3% current accuracy ensures consistent LED brightness and optimal LED lifetime by preventing over-current conditions.

coreAdvantages.title: Flexible Dimming Control
coreAdvantages.description: Support for both analog and PWM dimming provides flexibility for various control systems and smart lighting applications.

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Protection
coreAdvantages.description: LED open/short protection, over-temperature protection, and over-current protection ensure safe operation and long system lifetime.

coreAdvantages.title: Wide Input Range
coreAdvantages.description: 6V to 40V input range accommodates various power sources including AC-DC adapters, DC power systems, and battery supplies.

customerCases.challenge: The customer was developing a new line of LED downlights requiring high efficiency, compact size, and smooth dimming compatibility with standard wall dimmers. Their existing driver had poor dimming performance and generated excessive heat affecting LED lifetime.
customerCases.solution: We recommended the STR-L3201-based solution with optimized component selection for high efficiency. The design utilized PWM dimming for compatibility with standard dimmers and included proper thermal management. Our FAE team provided guidance on dimming circuit design and LED current setting.
result: Achieved 95% driver efficiency at full load
Smooth dimming from 100% to 5% without flicker
Driver temperature remained below 55°C in fixture
LED lifetime projection exceeded 50,000 hours
Product line successfully launched with 200K units sold
customerCases.feedback: The Starrystone Tech driver solved our dimming and thermal issues. LiTong's support in optimizing the design for our specific fixture was invaluable. The efficiency improvement significantly extended our product lifetime.

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed a driver solution for high-bay LED fixtures with multiple LED strings, requiring independent current control and high reliability for industrial environments. The solution needed to operate at elevated temperatures (up to 70°C ambient).
customerCases.solution: We designed a multi-channel solution using multiple STR-L3201 drivers, each controlling an LED string. The design included proper thermal management, over-temperature protection, and fault monitoring. Our team provided thermal simulation and layout optimization.
result: Successfully operated at 70°C ambient temperature
Independent current control for 4 LED strings
Overall system efficiency of 94%
Zero field failures in first year of deployment
Design adopted as standard for high-bay product line
customerCases.feedback: The multi-channel design with Starrystone Tech drivers met our demanding requirements. LiTong's thermal expertise ensured reliable operation in harsh industrial environments.

faeInsights.content: The STR-L3201 is an excellent choice for LED lighting applications requiring high efficiency and flexible dimming. The key to success with LED drivers is proper inductor selection and PCB layout. The inductor should be sized for the switching frequency (500kHz) with saturation current above the peak LED current. I recommend shielded inductors to minimize EMI. For dimming applications, PWM dimming is generally preferred over analog dimming because it maintains LED color temperature across the dimming range. The PWM frequency should be above 200Hz to avoid visible flicker, but not so high that it causes excessive switching losses. Pay attention to the current sense resistor - use 1% tolerance resistors for accurate current regulation, and ensure adequate power rating. The LED string voltage should stay within the driver's specifications (up to 36V for STR-L3201) and maintain sufficient headroom below the input voltage for proper buck operation.
faeInsights.logic: The LED driver design process follows this framework: First, determine LED string configuration (number of LEDs, forward voltage, current) based on light output requirements. Second, calculate input voltage requirements ensuring adequate headroom for buck operation. Third, select inductor value based on switching frequency and desired current ripple. Fourth, set current sense resistor for precise current regulation. Fifth, design dimming circuit based on control system requirements. Sixth, implement proper thermal management considering fixture constraints. Finally, validate through efficiency testing, thermal testing, and dimming performance verification.
keyTakeaways: Inductor selection is critical for efficiency and current ripple
PWM dimming maintains color temperature better than analog dimming
Current sense resistor tolerance affects brightness consistency
Thermal management is essential for long LED lifetime
Input voltage headroom ensures stable buck operation
commonPitfalls: Inadequate inductor saturation current causing instability
Insufficient input voltage headroom for buck operation
Poor thermal design reducing LED lifetime
Improper dimming frequency causing visible flicker
Inaccurate current setting causing brightness variation
bestPractices: Use shielded inductors with adequate saturation current margin
Maintain at least 2-3V headroom between input and LED string voltage
Design thermal management for worst-case ambient temperature
Use PWM dimming above 200Hz to avoid flicker
Use 1% tolerance current sense resistors for accurate regulation

faqs.question: How many LEDs can be driven with STR-L3201?
faqs.answer: STR-L3201 supports LED string voltage up to 36V. For typical white LEDs with 3V forward voltage, this allows up to 10-12 LEDs in series. For higher voltage LEDs or longer strings, ensure total LED voltage drop is below 36V. The input voltage must be at least 2-3V higher than the LED string voltage for proper buck operation. For example, with 12 LEDs (36V total), minimum input voltage should be 38-39V. For longer LED strings, consider the STR-H5601 boost driver which supports up to 60V output.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum 36V LED string (10-12 typical LEDs). Ensure adequate input voltage headroom.

faqs.question: What is the difference between analog and PWM dimming?
faqs.answer: Analog dimming varies the LED current proportionally to a control voltage (0-2.5V for STR-L3201). Lower current reduces brightness but may cause slight color temperature shift as LED characteristics change with current. PWM dimming switches the LED current on/off rapidly (typically 200Hz-1kHz) with variable duty cycle. PWM maintains consistent LED color temperature because LEDs operate at full rated current when on. Analog is simpler to implement with a potentiometer or DC voltage. PWM is preferred for color-critical applications and provides wider dimming range (down to 1% or lower).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PWM for color-critical applications and wide dimming range. Use analog for simple implementations.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the current sense resistor value?
faqs.answer: Calculate current sense resistor using: Rsense = Vcs / Iled, where Vcs is the current sense threshold voltage (0.25V for STR-L3201) and Iled is the desired LED current. For example, for 350mA LED current: Rsense = 0.25V / 0.35A = 0.714Ω. Use the closest standard value (0.68Ω or 0.75Ω). Calculate power dissipation: P = I² × R = (0.35A)² × 0.68Ω = 83mW. Use a resistor with at least 2x power rating (1/4W or 1/2W). Use 1% tolerance resistors for accurate current regulation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate using 0.25V sense threshold. Use 1% tolerance resistors with adequate power rating.

faqs.question: What inductor value should I use?
faqs.answer: For STR-L3201's 500kHz switching frequency, a 47μH inductor is recommended for most applications. The inductor should have saturation current rating at least 1.3-1.5 times the maximum LED current. For example, for 1A LED current, use inductor with 1.5A saturation current minimum. Low DCR (DC resistance) is important for efficiency - look for inductors with DCR below 100mΩ. Shielded inductors are recommended to minimize EMI. The datasheet provides detailed inductor selection guidelines for different current levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 47μH for typical applications. Ensure adequate saturation current rating.

faqs.question: How do I prevent LED flicker during dimming?
faqs.answer: To prevent visible flicker: Use PWM dimming frequency above 200Hz (human eye cannot detect flicker above this frequency); Avoid frequencies near 100-120Hz that can cause stroboscopic effects; Ensure stable input voltage to the driver; Use adequate output capacitance to smooth current; and Implement proper PCB layout to minimize noise. Some applications may require PWM frequency above 1kHz for professional photography or broadcast environments. The STR-L3201 supports PWM dimming up to several kHz.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PWM frequency above 200Hz. Test in actual application environment for flicker.

faqs.question: What happens if an LED fails open or short?
faqs.answer: STR-L3201 includes protection for both LED open and short conditions. If an LED opens (fails open circuit), the driver detects the over-voltage condition and enters protection mode to prevent output capacitor over-charging. The driver will shut down or enter hiccup mode. If LEDs short (fail short circuit), the driver limits current to prevent damage. In both cases, the driver protects itself and the remaining LEDs. Once the fault is cleared, normal operation resumes. These protections are automatic and require no external components.
faqs.decisionGuide: Protection is automatic. No external components needed. Driver protects itself and LEDs.
